title: 4-0: Cucurella starts in Spain
fetched: raw-HTML 直抓 + 抽取(jsonld:articleBody) + 清洗（逐字、未经任何模型/改写/翻译）
original_text:
Spain defeated Saudi Arabia in the second match of Group H in the World Cup, held in Atlanta. Cucurella started, played the entire match, and was involved in the plays that led to Spain's third and fourth goals.

Spain was dominant from the start and took the lead in the 10th minute through Lamine Yamal. In the 21st minute, Oyarzabal extended the lead, and three minutes later, he completed his brace in a move that involved Cucurella. The madridista passed the ball inside the box to Olmo, who assisted the forward to make it 3-0.

The fourth goal
After the restart, Spain scored their fourth goal with direct involvement from Cucurella. The defender connected with a powerful volley after a corner kick, which the goalkeeper saved, but the ball then deflected off Al Tambakti and went into the Saudi net to seal the 4-0 win (49’). Spain now has four points and will conclude this phase against Uruguay on June 27 (02:00 am).
